steps.title: Find the slope of line k
steps.content:
The line passes through (0,‑6) and (2,2). Use slope formula $$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$$.
Plug in: $$m = \frac{2 - (-6)}{2 - 0} = \frac{8}{2} = 4$$.

steps.title: Write the equation using slope‑intercept form
steps.content: The y‑intercept is given as (0,‑6), so b = –6. With slope m = 4, the line is $$y = 4x - 6$$.

steps.title: Evaluate at x = 20 to find w
steps.content:
Substitute x = 20: $$w = 4(20) - 6 = 80 - 6 = 74$$.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
